title: Mail
description: The SMTP server this site sends through. Invitations, notifications, and anything a plugin you have granted "mail:send" asks to send, all leave from here.
denied: You do not have permission to see this site's mail settings.
readOnly: You can see this configuration but not change it. That needs the settings:update permission.

test.subject: Z-CMS test email
test.body: This is a test email from Z-CMS. If you are reading it, this site's mail settings work.

server.hint: Your mail provider gives you these four. Port 587 with STARTTLS is the common case; 465 needs "implicit TLS" switched on.

sender.hint: Who the mail comes from. Plugins cannot change this — it is what stops a plugin sending as anyone it likes on your domain.

fields.enabledHint: Off keeps everything below saved, and refuses every send. The switch to reach for when mail is going somewhere it should not.
fields.secureHint: On for port 465. Off does not mean plaintext — the connection still upgrades with STARTTLS when the server offers it.
fields.passwordSet: A password is stored. Leave this blank to keep it, or type a new one to replace it.
fields.passwordUnset: No password stored. Leave blank if your server does not need one.
fields.replyToHint: Optional. Where replies go, when that is not the from address.

fromEnv.title: Using SMTP_* from the environment
fromEnv.body: Nothing has been saved for this site, so these values come from the environment — in development that is Mailpit, which captures mail instead of delivering it. Saving this form stores a configuration for the site and takes over from the environment.

actions.saveFailed: Could not save the mail settings.
actions.testSent: Test email sent to {to}. If it does not arrive, check the recipient's spam folder before changing anything here.
actions.testDenied: You do not have permission to send email from this site.
actions.saveFirst: Save the settings before sending a test — the test uses what is stored, not what is on screen.

plugins.none: No plugin on this site has been granted mail:send.
plugins.quota: Plugins may send at most {limit} emails per hour from this site, in total. The CMS's own mail — invitations, password resets — is not counted against it.
